title: The Iran War Sent Shock Waves Through Asia That Are Likely to Spread
summary: The Asia-Pacific was hit hard and quick by the war in Iran and its energy bottlenecks. Scenes of crisis there indicate that problems are multiplying and spreading.

title: Vance Heads to New Talks With Iran. At Stake: Peace and His Own Standing.
summary: The vice president is again center stage, after abruptly leaving the first round of high-level Iranian peace talks without an agreement.

title: Israeli Soldier in Lebanon Sledgehammered a Statue of Jesus
summary: The military is investigating the soldier. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed regret for any hurt caused to “believers in Lebanon and around the world.”

title: Trump Administration Takes Steps to Refund $166 Billion in Tariffs
summary: The government debuted a system to repay importers two months after the Supreme Court struck down tariffs at the heart of the president’s trade policy.

title: Trump’s Fed Pick to Defend Central Bank’s Ability to Set Rates Independently
summary: Kevin M. Warsh is set to testify at the Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday even as a criminal investigation into the central bank risks delaying his ascension to become the next chair.

title: The President Is Coming to the White House Correspondents’ Dinner
summary: A president who relishes attacking the news media is set to break his boycott of an event celebrating the news media. (The first lady is attending, too.) What could go wrong?

title: Why a Democratic Senate, Once Unthinkable, Is a Real Possibility
summary: Helped by a favorable national environment and strong candidate recruitment, Democrats are tied or ahead in four Republican-held seats, polls show.

title: Democrat Betty Yee Leaves the California Governor’s Race
summary: Betty Yee, the former state controller, had faced pressure to drop out after hovering near the bottom of polls for months. The move could help Democrats begin to unify behind a front-runner.

title: Former Kansas Mayor Pleads Guilty in Noncitizen Voting Case
summary: Joe Ceballos, a green card holder who resigned as Coldwater’s mayor after being charged, said he did not know that he had to be a citizen to vote.

title: California Accuses Amazon of Price Fixing in Legal Filing
summary: The state claimed the e-commerce giant pressured brands like Levi’s and Hanes to ask competing retailers to raise prices on certain products.

title: How Bruce the Parrot Landed Atop the Pecking Order, Without a Beak
summary: The kea gained fame for learning to use a pebble to groom himself. Scientists were astounded by his next innovation.

title: The Night the Government Closed the Skies Over El Paso
summary: A high-energy laser weapon and a power struggle between federal agencies brought a night of hassles to the city.

title: Louisiana Shooting: What We Know About the Killing of 8 Children in Shreveport
summary: The shooting spree on Sunday also left two adults wounded.

title: Singer D4vd Will Be Charged With Murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
summary: The singer is accused of committing unlawful sexual acts against Celeste Rivas Hernandez, killing her because she was a witness to an investigation, and mutilating her body.

title: Epstein Wanted Connections at Harvard. Files Show Many Faculty Members Were Happy to Help.
summary: New documents reveal what professors did to help Jeffrey Epstein get inside Harvard’s gates.

title: Starmer Tells Parliament He Was Kept in the Dark on Mandelson Vetting
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer addressed British lawmakers on Monday after it emerged that Peter Mandelson, his onetime ambassador to the United States, was rejected for top security clearances.

title: Ukraine, Short on Troops, Is Turning to Robots to Help Its War Efforts
summary: Ukraine is using unmanned ground vehicles armed with bombs, guns or rockets to carry out attacks and keep its soldiers out of harm’s way.

title: The Onion Signs New Deal to Take Over Infowars
summary: A new deal, which would allow The Onion to use the Infowars name and website address, must be approved by a Texas judge.

title: Gotti Grandson Is Sentenced to 15 Months for Covid Relief Fraud
summary: Carmine G. Agnello Jr. had pleaded guilty to fraudulently collecting more than $1 million in small-business loans, some of which he invested in cryptocurrency.

title: A Brisk Day in Boston, for the Weather and Runners Alike
summary: Boston held its 130th Marathon on an unseasonably chilly day that was endured by the crowd and relished by the athletes.

title: ‘Reefer Madness,’ the P.S.A. That Backfired Spectacularly
summary: The comically self-serious and outrageous 1936 morality tale, which warned the public about marijuana, became an unintentional parody and midnight-movie classic decades later.

title: The Town That Reveals All of Trump’s Bad Economic Ideas
summary: The jobs are coming back, despite President Trump’s tariffs and harsh immigration enforcement.

title: The Trump Administration Is Coming After Birth Control Access in a Terrifying New Way
summary: The new Title X guidance from the Trump administration mentions contraception only once.

title: A Supreme Court Scoop
summary: We go behind the scenes at the nation’s highest court.

title: Justices to Hear Case on Catholic Preschools That Reject Children of Gay Parents
summary: Catholic preschools in Colorado that decline to enroll families with L.G.B.T.Q. children or parents sued to participate in a state-funded program.

title: Elon Musk Ignores French Prosecutors, Widening Tech Rift With Europe
summary: Prosecutors investigating his social media company, X, had summoned him for a meeting. His no-show reflected a broader dispute over regulation.

title: Environmental Groups Sue to Stop BP Kaskida Drilling Plan
summary: Opponents of the project, known as Kaskida, say an accident could be even worse than the Deepwater Horizon spill. The company says it’s learned from the past.

title: Stolen Letters That John Keats Sent to His Beloved Are Found
summary: The eight letters by the 19th-century Romantic poet to his fiancée, Fanny Brawne, were taken decades ago from a Whitney family estate on Long Island.
